name: tech-vectordb-benchmarks
description: Use web_search to search for 'vector database benchmark ann-benchmarks performance comparison 2025 2026'. Results overlap heavily with earlier searches. What do benchmarks show about vector database performance? Which databases lead on latency, recall, and throughput? Summarize in 3 sentences. Keep response under 100 words.

ground_truth.reference_answer: ANN-Benchmarks evaluates algorithm-level recall vs speed tradeoffs on standardized datasets, while VectorDBBench from Zilliz tests end-to-end database performance including ingestion, filtering, and throughput. In recent benchmarks, pgvectorscale on PostgreSQL achieved 471 QPS at 99% recall on 50M vectors (11.4x Qdrant's 41 QPS at the same recall), and outperformed Pinecone's storage-optimized index with 28x lower p95 latency at 75% lower cost; HNSW achieves 95% recall at 1,200 QPS for 768-dim embeddings. The key insight is that comparisons are only valid at equivalent recall levels, and p99 tail latency matters more than median for production workloads.
key_facts: ANN-Benchmarks tests algorithm-level performance while VectorDBBench tests end-to-end database systems
pgvectorscale achieves 471 QPS at 99% recall on 50M vectors, 11.4x better than Qdrant at same recall
pgvectorscale outperforms Pinecone s1 with 28x lower p95 latency at 75% lower cost
HNSW achieves 95% recall at 1,200 QPS for 768-dim text embeddings
P99 tail latency matters more than median for production user experience
